items.item: Direct edits to 00_active_baseline from this Phase 9 package
items.reason: Phase 9 is harmonization readiness, not active-law amendment.
items.riskIfPromoted: Draft wording could become law without review.
items.allowedUse: Use proposal register and patch-proposal text for controlled review.

items.reason: Phase 8 tests are defined but not executed against an implementation.
items.riskIfPromoted: Users may trust an untested runtime.
items.allowedUse: State only that conformance gates are defined.

items.reason: OpenAPI/AsyncAPI surfaces are candidate contracts and not promoted/live.
items.riskIfPromoted: Application builders may rely on unstable endpoints.
items.allowedUse: Use as implementation scaffold and review input.

items.item: FMIS, machinery, or sensor import as accepted truth
items.riskIfPromoted: External source ambiguity could poison canonical truth.
items.allowedUse: Use candidate receipts, source-fidelity envelopes, and loss maps.

items.item: Minimum capture profile as default conformance
items.reason: Minimum capture is draft/non-default and needs owner/live evidence.
items.allowedUse: Use for pilots and ordinary-farm capture experiments under explicit boundaries.

items.item: Specific endpoint names as baseline law
items.reason: Endpoint names are draft carrier choices; semantic operations matter more than route syntax.
items.riskIfPromoted: Locks runtime design before implementation evidence.
items.allowedUse: Use route names as candidate OpenAPI examples.

items.reason: The package is still implementation-directed with bounded debt.
items.riskIfPromoted: Overstates maturity and invites premature governance dependence.
items.allowedUse: Use as predevelopment platform implementation support.

items.item: External API/tooling standards as OFARM semantic law
items.reason: OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, JSON Schema, FHIR, OPA, MCP, and HTTP patterns are carrier/tooling references only.
items.riskIfPromoted: External vocabulary could silently override OFARM semantics.
items.allowedUse: Use as implementation patterns subject to active baseline law.

items.item: Packs or public extensions mutating core meaning
items.reason: Pack law already forbids core-meaning mutation.
items.riskIfPromoted: Extension slots become hidden semantic forks.
items.allowedUse: Use explicit namespaced extensions that do not affect canonical truth unless governed.

items.reason: Agent manifests and hints are not authority decisions.
items.riskIfPromoted: AI tool metadata could become governance authority.
items.allowedUse: Use agent policy to route actions through OFARM authority preflight and approval gates.

items.item: Calculation formulas without evidence and versioning
items.reason: Numeric rules can affect governed outcomes.
items.riskIfPromoted: Hidden UI formulas or unreviewed defaults create inconsistent farm records.
items.allowedUse: Use formula catalog drafts and test vectors pending evidence.

items.reason: Labels help users but do not define OFARM states.
items.riskIfPromoted: App wording could collapse recommendation, prescription, claim, execution, correction, and dispute states.
items.allowedUse: Use UI-safe label matrix as presentation guidance only.
